lks2.objective: Work as a team to tackle the team in possession and pass the ball to the coach — coordination and pressure.
successCriteria: Closes down the player in possession
Looks up before passing to the coach

uks2.objective: Use coordinated team pressing to win possession, then pass accurately to the coach under control — combining defence and passing technique.
successCriteria: Communicates who presses and who covers
Passes with weight and accuracy to the coach
Recovers quickly when a teammate is dispossessed

equipment.qty: 1 per child in the defending team

space.description: Defined square area. Coach stands on the edge of the area where balls are delivered.

steps: Split into two teams. One team has a ball each (the defenders/possessors).
The other team has NO balls — they are the raiders.
Raiders aim to tackle the children with balls and PASS the won ball to the coach.
If a child is tackled, their team-mates can pass the ball to the coach instead.
Game continues until ALL balls have been passed to the coach.
progressions: Raiders can only tackle — not steal the ball outright (must pressure the dispossession).
Add a 2-touch rule for the team in possession — forces movement.
Coach moves position between rounds — forces raiders to look up before passing.
Add a second coach — pass to either.
questions: What's the best way to win the ball back as a team?
How did you know which raider to pass to the coach?
How do you support someone who's just been tackled?

step.space: Smaller area = quicker raids; larger = more dribbling and movement
step.task: Raiders must pass with weaker foot to score with the coach
step.equipment: Two coaches/targets at different sides — raiders pick the closest
step.people: Uneven teams — fewer raiders to make defending easier; more raiders to challenge possession

base.showmanship: Carefully select teams to ensure even ability. Encourage tackling AND accurate passing — celebrate when raiders make a clean tackle followed by a sharp pass to the coach.
